title: Characterizing Localization Differences Between WT and KO Fibroblasts
author: Eve Pourzan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rr9d596
materials: Add 300 microliters of blocking buffers to each well for 1 hour.
Make 1:200 dilution of primary antibodies (1ml of antibody dilution + 5 microliters of primary antibody)
Pipete up and down to resuspend cells
Cover plate with tinfoil to keep dark.
Using tool, pry off well walls.
View slides with EVOS using the manufacturers manual.

steps.title: Add 5 ml PBS to wash, and then aspirate
steps.instruction: Add 5 ml PBS to wash, and then aspirate

steps.title: Add 3 ml of Trypsin to flask
steps.instruction: Add 3 ml of Trypsin to flask

steps.title: Incubate for ~5 minutes to lift cells
steps.instruction: Incubate for ~5 minutes to lift cells

steps.title: Add 3 ml of EMEM media to stop trypsinization
steps.instruction: Add 3 ml of EMEM media to stop trypsinization

steps.title: Pipette up and down to clear all cells from plate
steps.instruction: Pipette up and down to clear all cells from plate

steps.title: Transfer all media and cells to new labeled tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer all media and cells to new labeled tubes

steps.title: Centrifuge (at 4000 rpm for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge (at 4000 rpm for 5 minutes) to create a cell pellet

steps.title: Add 1 ml of EMEM media
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml of EMEM media

steps.title: Label eppendorf tubes
steps.instruction: Label eppendorf tubes

steps.title: Pipette 10 microliters of Trypan Blue into each tube
steps.instruction: Pipette 10 microliters of Trypan Blue into each tube

steps.title: Add 10 microliters of designated cell...
steps.instruction: Add 10 microliters of designated cell solution into tubes with Trypan Blue

steps.title: Pipette up/down for good suspension
steps.instruction: Pipette up/down for good suspension

steps.title: Pipette 10 microliters of stained cell...
steps.instruction: Pipette 10 microliters of stained cell solution into a labeled Countess slide

steps.title: Use Countess Machine to count alive vs
steps.instruction: Use Countess Machine to count alive vs. dead cells. ** Use ZOOM function to adjust microscope EVERY TIME for an accurate count.

steps.title: For 8 well plate, we used 10,000 cells/ well
steps.instruction: For 8 well plate, we used 10,000 cells/ well. (20,000 cells/ ml) x (0.5 ml/well) = 10,000 cells/ well

steps.title: Use M1V1=M2V2 equation to determine master mix requirements
steps.instruction: Use M1V1=M2V2 equation to determine master mix requirements. OR you can use the Countess Count function.

steps.title: Knowing the cell dilution, make master mixes with determined amounts
steps.instruction: Knowing the cell dilution, make master mixes with determined amounts.

steps.title: Add 500 microliters of master mix...
steps.instruction: Add 500 microliters of master mix cell solutions to each well.

steps.title: Label and incubate for 24 hours
steps.instruction: Label and incubate for 24 hours.

steps.title: With existing media (which is 500...
steps.instruction: With existing media (which is 500 microliters), add 50 microliters of 37% formaldehyde to each well to give a 1:10 dilution.

steps.title: At this point, you can save...
steps.instruction: At this point, you can save slide in refridgerator or countinue with treatment.

steps.title: Make blocking buffer (0
steps.instruction: Make blocking buffer (0.5 g BSA powder + 30 microliters of Triton X-100 + 10 milliliters of PBS)

steps.title: Wash wells with 500 microliters of PBS
steps.instruction: Wash wells with 500 microliters of PBS.

steps.title: Set plate on room temperature rotating tray for 1 hour
steps.instruction: Set plate on room temperature rotating tray for 1 hour.

steps.title: Make antibody dilution (0
steps.instruction: Make antibody dilution (0.1g BSA powder + 30 microliters of Triton X-100 + 10 ml of PBS)

steps.title: Remove plate from rotating tray
steps.instruction: Remove plate from rotating tray

steps.title: Add 400 microliters of dilution to...
steps.instruction: Add 400 microliters of dilution to control wells and 400 microliters of dilution WITH antibodies to other wells.

steps.title: Incubate on rocker overnight in the cold room
steps.instruction: Incubate on rocker overnight in the cold room.

steps.title: Make secondary antibody dilution, if needed
steps.instruction: Make secondary antibody dilution, if needed. Can use extra stock from previous antibody dilution (

steps.title: g BSA + 30 microliters Triton...
steps.instruction: g BSA + 30 microliters Triton X-100 + 10 ml PBS)

steps.title: Make a 1:500 secondary antibody dilution
steps.instruction: Make a 1:500 secondary antibody dilution. (1 ml of antibody dilution + 2 microliters of secondary antibody).

steps.title: Keep secondary antibody dilution covered in foil
steps.instruction: Keep secondary antibody dilution covered in foil. Keep Dark!

steps.title: Wash wells with 500 uL PBS three times
steps.instruction: Wash wells with 500 uL PBS three times.

steps.title: Add 400 uL of secondary antibody dilution ot every well
steps.instruction: Add 400 uL of secondary antibody dilution ot every well.

steps.title: Incubate in room temperature on rocking tray for 1 hour
steps.instruction: Incubate in room temperature on rocking tray for 1 hour.

steps.title: Wash 3x with 500 uL of PBS
steps.instruction: Wash 3x with 500 uL of PBS.

steps.title: Add 1 small drop of DAPI to every well floor
steps.instruction: Add 1 small drop of DAPI to every well floor

steps.title: GENTLY place coverslip on top of DAPI drops
steps.instruction: GENTLY place coverslip on top of DAPI drops. DO NOT PRESS DOWN.

steps.title: Cover plate with foil/ and do...
steps.instruction: Cover plate with foil/ and do not disturb for 30+ minutes. The coverslip will adhese to the plate.
